description_long: This helicopter features a streamlined, angular fuselage with a prominent cockpit area and a long, tapered tail boom. The main rotor consists of two long, slender blades with yellow tips, while the tail rotor is mounted vertically at the end of the tail and features two short blades with orange tips. The cockpit is enclosed by large, dark-tinted windows that wrap around the front and sides, providing excellent visibility for the pilot and passengers. The helicopter rests on two sturdy, black landing skids connected by crossbars, designed for stable ground contact. The exterior is primarily a light gray color with black and orange accents on the rotors and skids. No visible registration numbers or markings are present on the fuselage.
description: A light gray, angular helicopter with large tinted cockpit windows, two-blade main and tail rotors, and black landing skids with orange accents.
description_view_0: Front view shows the wide cockpit windows, the main rotor blade extending horizontally, and the landing skids beneath the fuselage.
description_view_1: Left side view highlights the elongated tail boom, the vertical tail rotor, and the full profile of the cockpit and landing skids.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the tail boom narrowing to the tail rotor, the main rotor blade from above, and the symmetrical landing skids.
description_view_3: Right side view mirrors the left, showing the cockpit, tail boom, tail rotor, and landing skids from the opposite side.
attribute_rotor_blades: Two long, narrow main rotor blades, likely made of composite or metal, colored black with yellow tips, extending the full width of the helicopter.
attribute_tail_rotor: Vertical tail rotor with two short blades, colored black with orange tips, mounted at the end of the tail boom.
attribute_landing_skids: Two black, tubular landing skids with a simple, straight design and crossbar supports, likely made of metal or composite material.
attribute_cockpit_windows: Three large, dark-tinted cockpit windows wrapping around the front and sides, with angular framing and no visible markings.
